title: Integration tests must hit a real database, not mocks
description: Mocked the database in integration tests. The mocks passed cleanly; the production migration broke under real Postgres constraints. The bug had been catchable by a single real-DB test for weeks.
context: Adding a NOT NULL column with a backfill default; mocks didn't enforce constraint ordering the way Postgres does.
resolution: Rewrote the suite against a containerized Postgres; the failing case surfaced immediately.
prevention: Integration tests use a real database (containerized is fine). Unit tests can mock; integration tests cannot. If a test name contains the word 'integration', it must touch real infrastructure.
trigger: Writing or reviewing a test labelled 'integration' that imports a mocking library
